title: Meta AI — Wearables
role:
Senior Product Designer
AI Experiences Lead
credits:
Meta Reality Labs — Wearables AI
PM, Research, ML, and Hardware partners

hero.alt: Ray-Ban Meta companion app — Devices home with paired glasses, gallery, and the Ask Meta AI input.
hero.caption: Devices home in the companion app — paired glasses status, gallery, and the Ask Meta AI input.

brief: Lead designer for AI-powered experiences on Ray-Ban Meta. Defining how multimodal AI feels when you wear it: camera-aware vision, audio-first interactions, contextual prompts, and the seamless handoff between glasses and the companion app.

overviewMedia.alt: Field testing the camera-tap interaction in daylight.

body.heading: Problem
body.body: How do you make AI feel ambient — useful when you need it, invisible when you don't? Phones taught us to summon AI through chat windows. Glasses don't have a chat window. They have your eyes, your ears, and the world in front of you. The interaction model has to start over from first principles.

body.heading: Approach
body.body:
Started with field research with 30+ early adopters of Ray-Ban Stories and the original Meta-AI beta. Mapped the moments where AI is uniquely valuable on glasses (hands-free, eyes-up, in motion) vs. where it would just feel intrusive (in conversation, in flow, in privacy-sensitive contexts).

From there, ran weekly cross-functional working sessions with PM, Research, ML, and Hardware to translate those moments into product surfaces — camera tap, audio tap, voice wake, and the companion app's chat history.

media.alt: Voice-first response surfaces in the companion app.

body.heading: Solution
body.body:
A privacy-first interaction model where the user always initiates. Explicit camera tap, "Hey Meta" wake word, or app launch — never silent capture. Every AI response is logged in the companion app's chat thread so users can audit what their glasses heard, said, and saw.

The visual system is two-layer: the LED on the glasses (a deliberate hardware tell that AI is engaged) and a soft type/audio response that fades after delivery. No persistent UI on the lens, no glanceable cards. The AI is loud when invoked, silent the rest of the time.

media.alt: Cross-platform handoff between glasses and phone.
An AI-powered companion device — multimodal interaction across the wearable and phone.
media.caption: AI handoff between the wearable and the companion app.

body.heading: Reflection
body.body: Wearable AI is a fundamentally new design surface. The lessons here are about restraint as much as novelty: every milliwatt, every millisecond, every word the user has to say is a tax. The product wins by spending those resources very deliberately on the moments that actually matter to the wearer.
